title: fragaria vulgaris var. minor
intro: Fragaria vulgaris var. minor, also known as woodland strawberry or alpine strawberry, is a lesser-known variety of the common strawberry plant, characterized by its small size and white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, as it contains salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Fragaria vulgaris var. minor include its ability to aid in digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and even help alleviate symptoms of fever and headaches. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and is also used in herbal infusions and salads, showcasing its versatility in herbal medicine.
